publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 58-59. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W--. All is going on well, & Locock pays the very greatest attention & is kindness itself. The Hen is much better indeed for yesterday's bleeding & measures. She thinks she shall remain in Town until Locock goes abroad, which will be
in a fortnight. I think she will do wisely.
I believe I can probably leave her without fear on Saturday next. You & I might return together perhaps.
I wish, on some other accounts, to come to Town about the end of next week; but had only intended it to be for a morning. If however the Hen remains on in Town, I shall perhaps make it a day or two,
should I find reason to think I am wanted.
But unless she goes on very well now, I cannot leave her at present. She begins to fidget about my not being able to ride here.
As I am growing very very thin indeed; tho' I am well, in high spirits, & very happy. And Locock particularly prescribes riding for me. The Hen has wanted me to have my horses up; but I would

not of course. I am becoming such a little bag-of-bones, you cannot think. Mrs Grieg, whom I have had occasion to see, about some things of my mother's, was really quite shocked, altho' she said I look very brilliant in the face; but that my chest & figure seemed to be disappearing. But you know I like to be thin. So no matter; only people begin to look grave about it.-- I have
